title: Creamy Quick Malaysian Coconut Biryani
description: This is a quick, creamy, and flavorful Malaysian-inspired dish that features a mix of coconut milk and spices. A perfect solo dinner option that's gluten-free-friendly.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they are lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add the ground cinnamon, cardamom, cumin, and turmeric to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the uncooked rice to the skillet and stir to coat with the spice mixture, about 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the chopped nuts and coconut milk, and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the coconut milk has thickened slightly.
Fluff the biryani with a fork and serve hot.
